skill.name: avalonia-viewmodels-zafiro
skill.description: Create Avalonia ViewModels, wizards, navigation sections, and composition roots using Zafiro and ReactiveUI patterns.
skill.summary: Practical guidance for Avalonia MVVM patterns with Zafiro, ReactiveUI, navigation, and wizards.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported 78 potential issues, mainly shell backtick execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns. Manual review confirms these are false positives caused by Markdown code formatting, C# examples, and framework API names in documentation. No executable scripts, network calls, secret access, prompt injection, or malicious intent were found.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged Markdown inline code and fenced C# examples as Ruby or shell backtick execution. The reviewed locations contain API names and documentation examples, not executable shell commands or instructions to run untrusted input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Every reviewed location is Markdown prose or C# sample text. No file contains a Ruby script, shell command, process invocation, or command injection path.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Detections Are Keyword False Positives
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography on lines containing skill metadata, framework prose, or headings. No cryptographic API use, hashing, encryption, or password handling appears in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines do not call cryptographic functions or recommend weak algorithms. They are ordinary documentation text for Avalonia, Zafiro, ReactiveUI, and wizard patterns.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Detections Are Documentation False Positives
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged reconnaissance on documentation lines that mention framework lifecycle and disposal patterns. No commands, host enumeration, environment probing, or filesystem discovery instructions are present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious lines are C# lifecycle and cleanup examples. They do not inspect the system, read environment data, or collect host information.

content.value_statement: Avalonia teams need consistent patterns for reactive ViewModels, commands, wizards, and navigation. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to apply Zafiro and ReactiveUI conventions in C# desktop apps.
actual_capabilities: Explains ReactiveUI ViewModel structure using ReactiveObject, reactive properties, and observable transformations.
Describes enhanced command patterns with IEnhancedCommand, ReactiveCommand, and centralized error handling.
Guides multi-step flows with SlimWizard, WizardBuilder, validation gates, and final steps.
Shows navigation and section concepts using INavigator, IShellViewModel, and the Section attribute.
Documents composition patterns for DataTypeViewLocator, dependency injection, and view-to-ViewModel mapping.
limitations: It is documentation only and does not run code or modify projects by itself.
Examples are partial patterns that need adaptation to each Avalonia application.
It assumes familiarity with C#, MVVM, Avalonia, ReactiveUI, and dependency injection.
It does not verify package versions or compile generated code against a local solution.

use_cases.title: Create Reactive ViewModels
use_cases.description: Use the guidance to structure properties, observable state, commands, and disposal in Avalonia screens.

use_cases.title: Standardize Navigation Sections
use_cases.description: Apply consistent section registration and navigation patterns for tabbed or sidebar-based applications.

use_cases.title: Review Wizard Flows
use_cases.description: Compare multi-step workflow designs against SlimWizard patterns before implementation.

prompt_templates.title: Explain the Pattern
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain how this skill recommends building an Avalonia ViewModel with Zafiro and ReactiveUI. Keep the explanation practical.

prompt_templates.title: Design a ViewModel
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a ViewModel plan for an Avalonia form that validates input, exposes a submit command, and reports command errors.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Wizard Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a SlimWizard flow for creating a project. Include step responsibilities, validation gates, navigation behavior, and completion handling.

prompt_templates.title: Audit an Architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Avalonia MVVM architecture against Zafiro patterns. Focus on composition root design, ViewModel lifetimes, navigation, sections, and disposal.

output_examples.input: I need a ViewModel pattern for a settings form.
output: A recommended structure separates reactive state, validation, command creation, and error handling.
The submit action should be enabled only when the observed state is valid.
Subscriptions and command resources should be owned by a disposable scope.

output_examples.input: I need a three-step setup wizard.
output: The wizard plan starts with data entry, moves through validation, and finishes with a result step.
Each step owns one responsibility and exposes a clear navigation signal.
The final step should define whether the flow completes, saves, or deploys.

output_examples.input: Review my navigation approach for section-based screens.
output: Use section metadata for discoverable navigation entries.
Keep active section switching in the shell ViewModel.
Register section services in the composition root with appropriate lifetimes.

best_practices: Keep ViewModels reactive and isolate UI state from view-specific implementation details.
Centralize service registration and ViewModel lifetimes in the composition root.
Dispose observable subscriptions and command resources through a consistent disposable owner.
anti_patterns: Do not put navigation logic directly inside Avalonia views when a ViewModel can own the flow.
Do not create multi-step workflows as scattered event handlers across several screens.
Do not leave reactive subscriptions without a clear disposal strategy.

faq.question: What framework does this skill target?
faq.answer: It targets Avalonia applications that use Zafiro and ReactiveUI for MVVM structure, commands, navigation, and wizards.

faq.question: Does this skill execute commands?
faq.answer: No. The files are Markdown documentation with C# examples and do not execute shell commands or scripts.

faq.question: Can it generate complete working code?
faq.answer: It can guide code generation, but examples must be adapted and compiled in the target project.

faq.question: Is it useful without Zafiro?
faq.answer: Some ReactiveUI and MVVM ideas transfer, but the named patterns are designed for Zafiro APIs.

faq.question: What should I provide when asking for help?
faq.answer: Provide the target screen, command behavior, navigation flow, services, and any validation requirements.

faq.question: Does it cover Avalonia views?
faq.answer: It mainly covers ViewModels, navigation, wizards, composition, and view mapping rather than detailed XAML design.
